LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT SCRIPTDASH INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FAC DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS THAT ANY OFFICER, DIRECTOR OR MANAGING AGENT OF SCRIPTDASH ACTED WITH MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TOWARD EITHER OF THE PLAINTIFFS. IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO CORRECT THIS DEFICIENCY. OTHERWISE, IF THEY LEARN OF FACTS LATER IN THE CASE THEY CAN SEEK LEAVE TO AMEND TO REQUEST PUNITIVE DAMAGES. Off Calendar Aug-17-2018 Continued To Nov-07-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Scriptdash Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the first amended complaint is granted with twenty days leave to amend. The FAC does not sufficiently allege ultimate facts that any officer, director or managing agent of Scriptdash acted with malice, fraud or oppression toward either of the plaintiffs. If they can do so in good faith, plaintiffs are granted leave to correct this deficiency. Otherwise, if they learn of facts later in the case they can seek leave to amend to request punitive damages. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
